experiment: Pythia-6.9B mixed-data SAE seed-42 retrain audit trail
scope: Backs the README naming-notes claim that the Pythia-6.9B seed-42 SAE was retrained once and the suffix `_postfix` is preserved on disk for provenance. The original seed-42 SAE was trained at hyperparameters that did not match the rest of the cohort (multiplier 8, L1 1e-4); the postfix retrain produced a seed-42 SAE at the canonical cohort hyperparameters (multiplier 4, L1 5e-4) so that every seed in the N=5 cohort {42_postfix, 43, 44, 45, 46} shares an identical SAE training recipe. The retrain was a hyperparameter-conformance correction, not an outcome-driven retrain. Both runs are recorded here; the residual-above-reconstruction ordering is preserved across both.

hyperparameters.comment: Off-cohort hyperparameters; the rest of the N=5 cohort (seeds 43, 44, 45, 46) was trained at multiplier 4 / L1 5e-4.

comparison.note: Both runs show residual AUROC above SAE-reconstruction AUROC; the residual-above-reconstruction ordering is preserved across the hyperparameter change. The postfix retrain produces a tighter, more selective SAE (mean active features collapses from 551 to 11; recon cosine improves from 0.960 to 0.977) because the canonical cohort hyperparameters (multiplier 4 / L1 5e-4) target a sparser solution than the original (multiplier 8 / L1 1e-4). The narrative of the paper does not depend on which run is used; the residual carries membership signal that the reconstruction discards in both.

interpretation: A reviewer concerned that the postfix retrain was an outcome-driven choice (ie, dropping a SAE that did not show the residual-above-reconstruction effect) can verify directly from the pre_postfix_run.metrics block above that the original seed-42 SAE also showed residual_above_recon_pre=true with delta +0.159. Both runs support the paper's headline ordering.
